info.title: wcred
info.description: CLI tool for Windows Credential Manager. Securely store, retrieve, and manage credentials from the command line.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: list
commands.description: List stored credentials matching an optional filter. Shows target, user, type, and timestamps.

options.name: --wide
options.description: Show full details: target, user, type, last updated, and comment (if set) [default: False]

options.name: --columns
options.description: Target column width in table mode (default: 50) [default: 50]

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Wildcard filter pattern (e.g., 'git:*', 'MyApp:*'). Omit to list all credentials.

commands.name: get
commands.description: Retrieve and display a stored credential with all metadata

options.name: --show-password
options.description: Display the password in plain text (use with caution) [default: False]

commands.name: set
commands.description: Store or update a credential in Windows Credential Manager with optional metadata

options.name: --password
options.description: Password/secret value. If omitted, prompts interactively (recommended). Warning: command-line values are visible in shell history!

options.name: --persist
options.description: Storage persistence: Session (until logoff), LocalMachine (survives reboot, default), Enterprise (roams with domain profile) [default: LocalMachine]

options.name: --comment
options.description: Description or note (e.g., 'Production DB - rotate quarterly', 'Created by CI pipeline')

options.name: --type
options.description: Credential type: Generic (default, for apps), DomainPassword (Windows/NTLM auth), DomainVisiblePassword (RDP-style) [default: Generic]

options.name: --password-env
options.description: Name of an environment variable containing the password. Pass the NAME only, not the value (e.g., '--password-env MY_SECRET', not '--password-env $Env:MY_SECRET').

options.name: --password-stdin
options.description: Read the password/secret value from standard input. Prefer this over --password for better security. [default: False]

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Permanently remove a credential from Windows Credential Manager

options.name: --force
options.description: Skip confirmation prompt (use in scripts) [default: False]

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Target name of the credential to delete (case-sensitive, use 'list' to find exact names)

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export credentials to a JSON file with optional encryption. Useful for backup or migration.

options.name: --filter
options.description: Wildcard filter pattern to select credentials (e.g., 'git:*', 'MyApp:*'). One of --filter or --targets is required.

options.name: --targets
options.description: List of specific credential target names to export. One of --filter or --targets is required.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path for the exported JSON data.

options.name: --encrypt
options.description: Encrypt the exported credentials using password-based encryption. Without this, uses DPAPI encryption (current user only). [default: False]

options.name: --password
options.description: Password for encrypting the exported data. Required if --encrypt is set. Warning: command-line values are visible in shell history!

options.name: --password-env
options.description: Name of an environment variable containing the encryption password. Prefer this over --password for better security.

options.name: --password-stdin
options.description: Read the encryption password from standard input. Prefer this over --password for better security. [default: False]

options.name: --machine-scope
options.description: When using DPAPI encryption, use machine scope (accessible by all users on this machine). Default is user scope. [default: False]

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import credentials from a JSON file exported by this tool.

options.name: file
options.description: Path to the JSON file containing exported credentials.

options.name: --force
options.description: Overwrite existing credentials with the same target name. [default: False]

options.name: --password
options.description: Password for decrypting the imported data if it was password-encrypted. Warning: command-line values are visible in shell history!

options.name: --password-env
options.description: Name of an environment variable containing the decryption password. Prefer this over --password for better security.

options.name: --password-stdin
options.description: Read the decryption password from standard input. Prefer this over --password for better security. [default: False]

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Simulate the import without actually writing any credentials. [default: False]
